Air Traffic services at Edinburgh Airport (EDI/EGPH) are provided by ANSL not the UK’s main provider, NATS which is why disruption is limited to one airport. Several flights were due  [read more]

title: AvTalk Episode 348: Consider the airspace closed in its entirety
contentSnippet:

title: Edinburgh Airport Suspends All Flights Due to Air Traffic Control IT Failure
contentSnippet:
Edinburgh Airport has suspended all flights this morning, Friday 5 December, after an IT failure hit its air traffic control systems.
The post Edinburgh Airport Suspends All Flights Due to Air Traffic Control IT Failure appeared first on AviationSource News.

title: Qantas brings back its last A380 after half a decade
contentSnippet: VH-OQC “Paul McGinness”, which was mothballed in 2020 along with the rest of Qantas’ A380 fleet during the height of the COVID-19 pandemic, touched down in Sydney at 8:30am on Friday, having been in Abu Dhabi undergoing maintenance and refurbishments since 2021.

title: Delta Air Lines lands in Melbourne with new LA service
contentSnippet: Flight DL11 from LAX touched down at 7:30am on Friday aboard N527DN, the first A350-900 operated non-stop by a major American airline to Melbourne. Flights will operate three times per week on the 275-seater A350 fleet.

title: Skydiving instructors strike over ‘plummeting’ wages
contentSnippet: Instructors at eight sites across Queensland, New South Wales, and Victoria took industrial action on Friday, saying negotiations for Experience Co’s first-ever enterprise agreement have stalled for nearly 10 months, and that the company would “trade away the industry’s reputation for profit”.

title: ITA Airways to Launch Direct Flights from Rome to Houston
contentSnippet:
ITA Airways will commence direct services between Rome (FCO) and Houston (IAH) in May 2026, marking the first direct connection between the two hubs.
The post ITA Airways to Launch Direct Flights from Rome to Houston appeared first on AviationSource News.

title: AAIB Report: Fatal Spitfire MK 26B Accident at Enstone Airfield in July 2024
contentSnippet:
The UK AAIB has released its final report into the fatal crash of a Spitfire MK 26B replica at Enstone Airfield, Oxfordshire, on 28 July 2024.
